SPY $640.23 -0.16 (-0.0%)QQQ $567.69 -0.10 (-0.0%)VIX 29.44 -0.71 (-2.4%)GEX $-18002.7B -544.1B
Massive SPY put buying drives negative gamma while April skew collapses across major indices.
WHAT CHANGED
SPY gamma exposure plunged $544B negative to -$18T as traders bought 355K puts at 640/639 strikes for tomorrow's expiry
April skew compressed dramatically across SPY (+0.8vol, 0th percentile) and QQQ (+1.0vol, 0th percentile), signaling fear premium collapse
NVDA hit extreme negative gamma territory at -$79.9M (3rd percentile) with April skew also compressing +4.7vol
Multiple risk-on sectors showing maximum IV ranks: IBIT, TAN, XBI all at 100th percentile while VIX dropped 0.71 points
NOTABLE DIVERGERS
EWZ (emerging markets) and KWEB (China tech) hitting 99-100 IV ranks despite broader vol compression
Risk-off positioning in international exposure contrasts with domestic put accumulation

SPY $638.95 -3.50 (-0.5%)QQQ $566.92 -3.55 (-0.6%)VIX 29.69 +0.00 (+0.0%)GEX $-17469.8B -1560.4B
Skew compression across SPY, QQQ, and NVDA signals put protection demand despite flat VIX as negative gamma builds.
WHAT CHANGED
SPY and QQQ skew compressed to 0th percentile while indices drift lower, indicating heavy put buying overwhelming call activity
Negative SPY gamma accelerated $1.6T deeper to -$17.5T, creating unstable positioning ahead of potential moves
NVDA hit extreme negative gamma territory at -$79.9M (3rd percentile), suggesting concentrated hedging despite tech strength narrative
Large put block flow in SPY 640P and 690P strikes totaling 119K contracts shows institutional downside protection ramping
NOTABLE DIVERGERS
IBIT, TAN, XBI all at 100th IV percentile rank despite crypto/growth sector resilience
EWZ and KWEB near max IV ranks signaling emerging market stress diverging from US equity calm
The combination of compressed skew, extreme negative gamma, and concentrated put buying suggests dealers are short volatility into a potentially unstable setup, despite VIX remaining flat at 29.69.
